meaning: adv.不能挽回地,不能补救地
examples: Eventually her marriage broke down irretrievably.
Some of our old traditions are irretrievably lost.
By then it will be irretrievably too late.
Clearly, the private-to-public hand off that has worked well for so many years is irretrievably broken.
Toward the end, illness ravaged his voice, and he was terrified that it had been irretrievably stolen from him.
